summary: In high-copper amalgams, \(\epsilon\) represents an alloy formed by specific interactions between copper and tin, most commonly denoted as Cu6Sn5, which provides desirable mechanical properties suitable for dental use despite not being directly listed among standard options here. This compound enhances strength while maintaining good workability during placement. ```plaintext Summary: In high-copper amalgams, ε denotes the Cu6Sn5 phase, providing enhanced mechanical properties crucial for effective tooth restoration. ```
